regulations.notes: Red drum (puppy drum). 1 per person/day. Any harvested red drum must be reported at deq.nc.gov/report-my-fish.

regulations.slotDescription: 14–20" total length, plus one over 26" allowed
regulations.notes: Spotted seatrout. 3 per person/day. NCDMF may close the season by proclamation after a major cold-stun — check for active proclamations.

seasonClosures.label: Recreational harvest closed statewide (a short season may open by NCDMF proclamation)

regulations.notes: Southern flounder recreational harvest is closed under the standing rules. In recent years NCDMF opened only a brief season (about two weeks in September) by proclamation. Verify the current flounder proclamation before keeping any.
Largemouth (black) bass statewide default. Many specific reservoirs and rivers set larger limits — check the waterbody.
No statewide size or creel limit. Specific waters set their own (e.g. Shearon Harris: 8" minimum, 20/day). Check the waterbody.
Blue, channel, and flathead catfish have no statewide size or creel limit on most waters. Some listed waters (certain ponds, reservoirs) restrict take — check the waterbody.
Bream/sunfish have no statewide size or creel limit. Some waters east of I-95 cap 30/day (no more than 12 redbreast).
